title: Pumpkin — variety-driven spacing and harvest timing
body: HORDI prescribes variety-specific spacing for pumpkin because days-to-harvest differs dramatically between types. Local strains plant at 3 × 3 m and are harvested 60 days after flowering. A.N.K. plants tighter at 2.5 × 2.5 m and is picked 40 days after flowering. Butternut is essentially a trellis / dense-bed crop at 50 × 100 cm and is ready 20 days after flowering. All three share the same basal of 220 Urea + 380 TSP + 125 MOP (kg/ha) plus two light top-dressings of 45 Urea + 25 MOP at 4 and 8 weeks. Planting at the wrong spacing for the variety halves effective yield because competition for light and moisture pulls down fruit set.
quote: Local Strain: 3 × 3m; A.N.K.: 2.5 × 2.5m; Butternut: 50 × 100cm. Harvest Timing — A.N.K.: 40 days after flowering; Local Variety: 60 days after flowering; Butternut: 20 days after flowering.
